title: US regulators say tokenized securities subject to same capital rules
summary: The Federal Reserve and US banking regulators have clarified that tokenized securities are subject to the same capital treatment as traditional assets.

title: Dems plan bill to curb prediction markets after ‘very specific’ Iran strike bets
summary: Senator Chris Murphy says it’s likely people close to Donald Trump with “inside information” made bets on prediction markets on when the US would strike Iran.

title: Construction begins at quantum facility big enough to break Bitcoin
summary: PsiQuantum co-founder Terry Rudolph said in July it has no plans to attack Bitcoin, even if its upcoming facility becomes powerful enough to break the blockchain’s cryptography.

title: SEC ends case against Justin Sun with $10M settlement
summary: The US SEC ended its lawsuit against crypto entrepreneur Justin Sun after one of his companies agreed to pay a $10 million settlement, closing a three-year legal battle.

title: Ether’s path to $2.5K may be trickier than expected: Here’s why
summary: Crypto markets spent the week chasing green, but Ether’s rally toward $2,500 might hit significant setbacks. Cointelegraph explains why.

title: IRS proposes mandating electronic delivery of tax forms for crypto
summary: If adopted, the proposal will take effect on Jan. 1 of the calendar year following the publication of the final IRS rules.

title: Bitcoin miners offload 15K BTC since October, with more sales expected
summary: Public miners are trimming Bitcoin reserves as tightening margins, debt pressure and a post-crash reset force the industry to rethink its once-popular hold strategy.

title: Bitcoin fails again at $71,500 as weakening momentum raises risk of a deeper pullback
summary: Bitcoin has again failed to hold $71,500, reinforcing the level as a long-term ceiling while global markets shift into a risk-off environment driven by rising oil prices and higher bond yields. The latest rejection came after Bitcoin briefly rose past $73,000, then lost momentum and fell back below

title: Israel’s Iran war will soon cost the equivalent of 41,300 Bitcoin every week
summary: Israel’s Finance Ministry has put a weekly price tag on the country’s widening war with Iran, estimating that the economy could take a hit of more than 9 billion shekels (equivalent to $2.93 billion) a week if emergency limits on activity remain in place. The estimate links the economic toll to the

title: Ripple quietly appears inside Wall Street’s stock-clearing system as it expands XRP payments platform
summary: Ripple is sharpening its argument that it can help institutions move value across traditional rails, stablecoins, and blockchain networks. On March 2, DTCC’s National Securities Clearing Corporation updated its MPID directory to add Ripple-owned “Hidden Road Partners CIV US LLC” for its first trade.

title: Crypto platform aims to let retail investors buy IPO shares at the same price as Wall Street insiders
summary: Most people only see IPOs after the price resets at the open. Institutional investors and select clients receive allocations at the offering price, while everyone else waits for the exchange to start trading and buys at the market price.
